text.title: fco-rightsizing Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for fco-rightsizing. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: Before rightsizing any production instance, collect at minimum 4 weeks of CPU and memory utilization data, identify peak values (not just averages), and implement the change in non-production first with a 48-hour validation window before promoting to production.
rule.rationale: Averages hide spikes. A 15% average CPU with a 95th-percentile of 85% is NOT a rightsizing candidate. The 4-week window captures weekly patterns including batch jobs, end-of-month processing, and marketing campaigns.

rule.statement: Migrate all EBS GP2 volumes to GP3 during the next maintenance window; GP3 costs 20% less and provides higher baseline IOPS (3,000 vs 100 for GP2) without requiring size changes.
rule.rationale: GP3 is strictly better than GP2 in both price and baseline performance. The migration requires no instance restart and no application changes.
